advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Scope the test assertion to the optionality job's use of the refspec (e.g. assert the `gerrit-refspec: ${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}` line appears within the parsed `optionality` job block) rather than a bare `"GERRIT_REFSPEC" in gerrit_yml`.
evidence: gerrit-verify.yaml:34: `GERRIT_REFSPEC:  { description: "Gerrit refspec ...", required: true, type: string }`
gerrit-verify.yaml:135: `gerrit-refspec: ${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}` (build-and-test job, unrelated to optionality)
Docstring claims it locks that 'gerrit-verify invokes the reusable optionality workflow with the Gerrit refspec' but the substring check is satisfied by pre-existing, unrelated occurrences.
advisory.finding: The new test's assertion `assert "GERRIT_REFSPEC" in gerrit_yml` does not actually verify that the *optionality* job passes the refspec; `GERRIT_REFSPEC` already appears in gerrit-verify.yaml independently (the workflow input definition at line 34, and in require-ticket line 72 and build-and-test line 135). Deleting `gerrit-refspec: ${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}` from the optionality job — the exact silent-false-PASS-on-main regression the docstring warns about — leaves the test green.
advisory.suggested_fix: Parse gerrit-verify.yaml (yaml.safe_load), locate jobs['optionality'], and assert its `with.gerrit-refspec` equals `${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}`, so the assertion is bound to the optionality job specifically.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the adjacency substring match with a parse that asserts `optionality` is a member of jobs['vote']['needs'].
evidence: 110:         "build-and-test, optionality" in gerrit_yml or "optionality, build-and-test" in gerrit_yml
advisory.finding: The `vote`-needs-optionality check uses a brittle exact-substring match `"build-and-test, optionality" in gerrit_yml or "optionality, build-and-test" in gerrit_yml`. It only tolerates a flow-style list with those two entries adjacent; a valid reformat (block-style `needs:` list, reordering with other entries between them, or extra whitespace) would break the test even though the wiring remains correct.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the intentional deviation from the ticket's smart-checkout composite is acceptable, and consider deduplicating the 4× inlined conditional-checkout block (e.g. a matrix/anchor pattern or accept the duplication with a note).
evidence: Ticket 15e7-f013-0cfb-494b file_impact lists `.github/actions/smart-checkout/action.yml` — 'new composite: branch-head vs gerrit-patchset checkout'
Commit message: 'The composite-action option was dropped: a local ./.github/actions/... action cannot perform the initial checkout ... so the conditional checkout is inlined per job.'
_optionality.yml:45-54, 70-79, 139-148, 180-189: the same conditional checkout block is duplicated across all four jobs.
advisory.finding: The delivered diff drops the `.github/actions/smart-checkout/action.yml` composite action that the ticket's Scope and file_impact explicitly enumerate, inlining a per-job conditional checkout instead. The commit message gives a sound rationale (a local composite action cannot perform the initial repo checkout), but the change diverges from the recorded plan/AC.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Scope the GERRIT_REFSPEC assertion to the optionality job block (or assert the exact `uses: ./.github/workflows/_optionality.yml` block passes `gerrit-refspec: ${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}`) so removing the refspec from the optionality job fails the test.
evidence: gerrit-verify.yaml:34 declares the input: `GERRIT_REFSPEC:           { description: "Gerrit refspec ...", required: true, type: string }`
gerrit-verify.yaml:72 and :135 already use `gerrit-refspec: ${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}` in pre-existing jobs (require-ticket, build-and-test)
ABSENCE: no assertion scopes the check to the `optionality:` job block, so removal of line 330 (`gerrit-refspec: ${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}`) is undetectable
advisory.finding: The assertion `assert "GERRIT_REFSPEC" in gerrit_yml` cannot detect the regression it claims to guard: `GERRIT_REFSPEC` is a pre-existing workflow input (gerrit-verify.yaml:34) already used by the require-ticket (line 72) and build-and-test (line 135) jobs, so the substring is true independent of the new optionality job. Deleting `gerrit-refspec: ${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}` from the optionality job (line 330) — producing the branch-head/main 'silent false PASS' the docstring warns about — leaves this assertion green.
scenarios: A future edit drops the refspec input from the optionality job; the reusable workflow checks out branch head/main instead of the patchset; the gate green-lights a leak on the actual patchset
workflow_dispatch run where a plain checkout resolves to main
advisory.suggested_fix: Parse the `optionality:` job region and assert the refspec wiring within it, e.g. locate the block after `uses: ./.github/workflows/_optionality.yml` and assert `gerrit-refspec: ${{ inputs.GERRIT_REFSPEC }}` appears in that block, rather than grepping the whole file for the bare token.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an assertion that `_optionality.yml` exists and contains the core check signatures (e.g. clean-core-wheel / per-extra / union job or command markers) so gutting/removing the reusable workflow fails the parity test.
evidence: tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py:98,117 only assert `_REUSABLE_OPTIONALITY in gerrit_yml` / `in optionality_yml` (the two caller files)
ABSENCE: the test never reads `_optionality.yml`; `_REUSABLE_OPTIONALITY` is a bare path string, not a `Path` that is read/asserted to exist
_optionality.yml:40+ holds the real checks (clean-core-wheel, per-extra, union) that no assertion touches
advisory.finding: No assertion verifies the reusable `_optionality.yml` exists or contains the actual checks; all four assertions only grep the two CALLER files for the string path. Deleting or gutting the reusable workflow leaves both callers referencing a now-empty/absent path, the test stays green, and the entire clean-core-wheel/per-extra/union contract silently stops gating — the exact regression this change was created to prevent.
scenarios: `_optionality.yml` is renamed/deleted but caller references are not updated (or updated to a stub) — GitHub Actions would error at runtime, but the parity test that is supposed to lock the wiring passes
A refactor empties the reusable workflow's jobs while keeping the file
advisory.suggested_fix: Read `_ROOT / '.github' / 'workflows' / '_optionality.yml'` and assert it exists plus contains stable signatures for the four checks (e.g. `clean core wheel`, `per-extra`/`matrix`, `union`), mirroring the `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` approach already used in this module.

coaching.coaching: Delete the change-detector / tautological test the GERRIT_REFSPEC presence assertion; do not re-pin it to the new source text — a test that only mirrors the implementation asserts nothing.
coaching.subject: the GERRIT_REFSPEC presence assertion
